perturbation.original_text: Each party shall utilize the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party only for the purpose of the Discuss ions and shall not divulge it to others or utilize it for commercial use or practice or for any other purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the d isclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall utilize the Confidential Data disclosed by the other party only for the purpose of the Discuss ions and shall not divulge it to others or utilize it for commercial use or practice or for any other purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the d isclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" introduces a new term that is undefined, thereby creating a conflict. Throughout the document 'Confidential Information' is consistently used with the defined meaning in section 1. Using 'Confidential Data' instead without defining it creates uncertainty whether it should follow the already defined 'Confidential Information' or if it has a different and new meaning altogether. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purpose of Disclosure . Each party shall utilize the Confidential Data disclosed by the other party only for the purpose of the Discuss ions and shall not divulge it to others or utilize it for commercial use or practice or for any other purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the d isclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in the Changed Text introduces a new, undefined term. The Contradicted Text also uses the term 'Confidential Data', implying it has a meaning within the contract. However, 'Confidential Information' is consistently defined and used throughout the document. Therefore, the introduction of 'Confidential Data' creates a strong in-text contradiction and uncertainty as to whether 'Confidential Data' is synonymous with 'Confidential Information', or has a different meaning, thus creating a conflict as the Misaligned Terminology definition describes.

perturbation.original_text: The party to whom disclosure of the Confidential Information has been made (the “receiving party”) shall treat such information as confidential and shall exercise reasonable care with respect to the Confidential Information, at least to the same degree of care as the party employs with respect to protecting its own proprietary and confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The party to whom disclosure of the Confidential Information has been made (the “receiving party”) shall treat such information as confidential and shall exercise reasonable care with respect to the Proprietary Data, at least to the same degree of care as the party employs with respect to protecting its own proprietary and confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Proprietary Data" creates confusion because 'Proprietary Data' is not previously defined. This implies that what the receiver should protect is not necessarily 'Confidential Information' as defined in the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction because 'Proprietary Data' may have its own definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Precautions . The party to whom disclosure of the Confidential Information has been made (the “receiving party”) shall treat such information as confidential and shall exercise reasonable care with respect to the Proprietary Data, at least to the same degree of care as the party employs with respect to protecting its own proprietary and confidential information. A receiving party shall limit disclosure of Confidential Information to those directors, officers, employees, and agents of the party who need to know the Confidential Information in connection with the Discussions and have been advised of the confidential nature of the information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' introduces a contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text both refer to 'Proprietary Data' and indicate it should be protected, but 'Proprietary Data' is not defined. This creates ambiguity and contradicts the implicit understanding that the receiver should protect 'Confidential Information' as originally stated and defined elsewhere, aligning with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not include and this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is at the time of disclosure or later becomes known to the public through no fault of the receiving party; (b) has been furnished or made known on a non- confidential basis to the receiving party by a third party who has a lawful right to disclose such information; (c) was known by the receiving party prior to its receipt as shown by competent evidence of the receiving party; (d) is required by law or court order to be disclosed, but only to the extent of such required disclosure; or (e) is independently developed by the receiving party without reference to or use of the other party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Information shall not include and this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is at the time of disclosure or later becomes known to the public through no fault of the receiving party; (b) has been furnished or made known on a non- confidential basis to the receiving party by a third party who has a lawful right to disclose such information; (c) was known by the receiving party prior to its receipt as shown by competent evidence of the receiving party; (d) is required by law or court order to be disclosed, but only to the extent of such required disclosure; or (e) is independently developed by the receiving party without reference to or use of the other party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Proprietary Information" in the section that defines exceptions to confidentiality creates a significant in-text contradiction. This makes it unclear whether the exceptions apply to all 'Confidential Information' as previously defined, or only to information also considered 'Proprietary Information'. This shift in terminology introduces ambiguity about what information is truly protected and could render the exceptions open to broad interpretation, contradicting the established framework.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exceptions . Proprietary Information shall not include and this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is at the time of disclosure or later becomes known to the public through no fault of the receiving party; (b) has been furnished or made known on a non- confidential basis to the receiving party by a third party who has a lawful right to disclose such information; (c) was known by the receiving party prior to its receipt as shown by competent evidence of the receiving party; (d) is required by law or court order to be disclosed, but only to the extent of such required disclosure; or (e) is independently developed by the receiving party without reference to or use of the other party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines exceptions to 'Confidential Information.' The changed text redefines this section using 'Proprietary Information' instead of 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text continues to use both terms. This introduces a contradiction because it becomes unclear which information the exceptions apply to, causing confusion and potentially conflicting obligations, aligning with the definition of misaligned terminology leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosing Confidential Information pursuant to the terms of this Agreement creates no ownership or license rights in the receiving party, and the disclosing party reserves all patent, trade secret and other proprietary rights it m ay have in such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing Classified Information pursuant to the terms of this Agreement creates no ownership or license rights in the receiving party, and the disclosing party reserves all patent, trade secret and other proprietary rights it m ay have in such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Classified Information" in the section regarding ownership creates a conflict. The term 'Classified Information' has a completely new implication than just confidential, therefore this can create a completely new level of restrictions and obligations. This introduction of 'Classified Information' without any context of definition leads to confusion and directly impacts how the agreement is interpreted and if it's being interpreted in the way that was originally intended. This creates in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership . Disclosing Classified Information pursuant to the terms of this Agreement creates no ownership or license rights in the receiving party, and the disclosing party reserves all patent, trade secret and other proprietary rights it m ay have in such Confidential Information. 8. Return of Confidential Information . In the event the business relationship between the parties is not established or is terminated for any reason, either voluntarily or involuntarily, the parties shall, if either party so requests in writing, promptly return to the other party, or destroy, all written data and documents, including originals, copies, translations and reproductions thereof, whether on paper or in electronic form, embodying all or part of the Confidential Information dis closed by the other party, as directed by the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Classified Information' in the section regarding ownership. The explanation states that 'Classified Information' introduces a completely new level of restrictions and obligations without any definition, which leads to confusion and directly impacts how the agreement is interpreted. The Contradicted Text uses both 'Classified Information' and 'Confidential Information' implying a difference and creating a contradiction because 'Classified Information' is undefined. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The change introduces a new term, 'Classified Information', which impacts the interpretation of the 'Return of Confidential Information' clause.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent that Confidential Information, as defined herein, is subject to U.S. export control laws and regulations under 22 CFR § 120 et. s eq. of the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (“ITAR”) and 15 CFR § 730 et. seq. of the Export Administration Regulations (“EAR”), and by entering into this Agreement, the parties agree that they will not v iolate any laws and/or regulations under ITAR and EAR, and that they will not, without limitation, disclose, transfer, or export Confidential Information to third parties, including foreign persons or entities , whether or not related to or affiliated with such party, and/or subcontract out any work and/or orders arising from this Agreement, without first receiving express written consent from the disclosing party and as required by contract or by law, each party shall register with the Office of the Directo rate of Defense Trade C ontrols (“DDTC”) .
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent that Sensitive Information, as defined herein, is subject to U.S. export control laws and regulations under 22 CFR § 120 et. s eq. of the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (“ITAR”) and 15 CFR § 730 et. seq. of the Export Administration Regulations (“EAR”), and by entering into this Agreement, the parties agree that they will not v iolate any laws and/or regulations under ITAR and EAR, and that they will not, without limitation, disclose, transfer, or export Confidential Information to third parties, including foreign persons or entities , whether or not related to or affiliated with such party, and/or subcontract out any work and/or orders arising from this Agreement, without first receiving express written consent from the disclosing party and as required by contract or by law, each party shall register with the Office of the Directo rate of Defense Trade C ontrols (“DDTC”) .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Information" at the beginning of the Export Control section creates a critical ambiguity and contradiction. The section then proceeds to discuss regulations regarding 'Confidential Information'. The change to 'Sensitive Information' raises doubts about which information the export controls truly apply to—only the sensitive type, or also the general confidential information as defined in the document. Because this new term doesn't replace "Confidential Information" everywhere, it causes contradiction when it comes to disclosing, transferring, or exporting the information. What happens now? This creates potential for disputes and undermines the purpose of the export control clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Export Control Law Compliance . To the extent that Sensitive Information, as defined herein, is subject to U.S. export control laws and regulations under 22 CFR § 120 et. s eq. of the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (“ITAR”) and 15 CFR § 730 et. seq. of the Export Administration Regulations (“EAR”), and by entering into this Agreement, the parties agree that they will not v iolate any laws and/or regulations under ITAR and EAR, and that they will not, without limitation, disclose, transfer, or export Confidential Information to third parties, including foreign persons or entities , whether or not related to or affiliated with such party, and/or subcontract out any work and/or orders arising from this Agreement, without first receiving express written consent from the disclosing party and as required by contract or by law, each party shall register with the Office of the Directo rate of Defense Trade C ontrols (“DDTC”) .
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Information" in the changed text creates a contradiction with the subsequent reference to "Confidential Information" within the same section and within the Contradicted Text section. The Contradicted Text section references 'Confidential Information' when discussing export regulations, while the changed text introduces 'Sensitive Information' without fully replacing all instances of the original term. This misalignment, as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', introduces ambiguity about which type of information is subject to export control, creating a direct conflict in how the export control clause should be interpreted and enforced.
